svg.aria-label: J3nna Mesh — a loosely-coupled peer-to-peer agentic and service mesh, with trust built in. The animation walks six steps of the agent lifecycle: enroll, discover, call tools, rooms, memory and secrets, and revoke. A console is the root of trust that issues grants and then steps off the hot path. Disparate specialized agents — your own agents plus the bundled roles room-agent, room-view and signal-bridge — announce a signed presence, discover each other over multicast and gossip, and cooperate peer-to-peer over the jip protocol in a shared room, bridging events outside, with no broker on the data path. Agents can embed the optional kernel for memory and seal secrets in a vault used by handle.
svg.text: A loosely-coupled peer-to-peer agentic & service mesh — with trust built in

g.text: Disparate agents find one another and cooperate directly — no broker on the data path.
Enroll once — the authority signs a grant, then steps off the path.
Discover — agents announce a signed presence and find each other over multicast + gossip.
Call tools — peer-to-peer, verified offline; restricted calls carry a signed CallProof.
Rooms — humans and agents collaborate in a shared, hosted space.
Memory & secrets — recall ⇆ remember in the optional kernel; secrets sealed in the vault, used by handle.
Revoke — pull a grant and the peer’s path goes dark; it drops from the whole mesh in seconds.
